heroHeadline: ICE Custody Deaths Hit 29 in FY2026, Breaking 22-Year Record; Former Judges Condemn Court Purge
heroSubtitle: ICE released delayed death reports April 18 as fiscal year 2026 total surpasses the 2004 record of 28; calendar year deaths at 17 including Cuban national Aled Carbonell-Betancourt at Miami detention, presumed suicide; former immigration judge Ryan Wood says administration wants 'numbers, they want deportations' — judges 'walked off the bench mid-decision'; acting director Todd Lyons announces resignation effective May 31 after overseeing record-breaking enforcement and custody deaths
footerNote: Data sourced from DHS, ICE official records, ACLU, HRW, DHS OIG, TRAC Reports, Detention Watch Network, court records, and Congressional records
